what_it_does: Standalone acceptance test suite for the additive `json_schema` transport branch in `fabric/transport.py`. It unit-checks request-body shaping, live-checks a resident vLLM at :8000 for schema acceptance, preserves the existing `json=True` path, verifies `client.complete()` remains the validation/enforcement layer, and tests finish_reason/usage passthrough through a new `meta` out-param.
observations: Self-bootstraps the repo root onto `sys.path` so the file can run standalone.
Uses global `_passed`/`_failed` lists and simple `ok()`/`bad()` helpers rather than a test framework.
Defines a `_capturing_transport` to assert exact request bodies without network calls.
Includes a negative-control live prompt that asks for plain English while still passing `json_schema`, to detect whether the backend is actually constraining decode.
Writes a structured evidence dict to `/tmp/ltransport_evidence.json` for downstream reporting.
Hardcodes resident model `cyankiwi/Qwen3.5-4B-AWQ-4bit` and base URL `http://127.0.0.1:8000/v1`.
Mixes unit assertions and live network calls in one file, skipping live checks if the server is unreachable.
standouts: The negative-control live test that tries to distinguish prompt compliance from true backend-constrained decoding.
The additive `meta={}` out-param design for returning finish_reason and usage without changing the main return type.
The capturing-transport pattern used to assert exact request shape without making HTTP requests.
conventions: Follows an acceptance-suite pattern with hand-rolled pass/fail tracking and a top-of-file docstring explaining lane IDs.
Uses `urllib` directly for live checks and reads HTTPError bodies to distinguish failure modes.
Keeps tests runnable as `python tests/json_schema_transport_acceptance.py` via `sys.path` manipulation.
concerns: Hardcoded `/tmp` output path and hardcoded resident server URL/model reduce portability.
Global mutable state (`_passed`, `_failed`) and broad `except Exception` blocks reduce isolation and diagnostic precision.
Live tests depend on an external resident 4B server being up at :8000.
The negative-control non-conformance is intentionally not a failure, which could be misread as a skipped assertion.
notes: A fail-loud acceptance harness that proves the additive json_schema transport branch is correctly shaped, accepted by the resident vLLM, backward-compatible with json_object, and observable via a side-channel meta dict.
questions: What is the exact implementation of `fabric.transport._apply_response_format` and `_fill_meta`?
Does `client.complete()` already accept `json_schema` and `meta` keyword arguments?
What is the `FocusOut` schema and where is it defined?
How is the resident vLLM server expected to be launched for these tests?
What do the lane identifiers O3, F9, R1-FOLD, C1.4 refer to in this project?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose in the docstring lists five goals, and the file implements tests for each of them, so purpose matches actual behavior.
apparent_themes: Observability passthrough without breaking caller contracts
intention_signals: The file is trying to demonstrate that the new json_schema transport branch is a safe, additive decode-strengthening layer: it shapes requests correctly, works against a real vLLM, leaves existing json_object callers untouched, keeps complete() as the enforcement point, and adds finish_reason/usage metadata without perturbing legacy callers.
novelty: The negative-control live prompt to detect true backend-guided decoding, and the `meta` out-param pattern for returning auxiliary response envelope data without changing function return types.
contribution: A runnable acceptance suite that captures expected request shapes, exercises live vLLM json_schema and json_object paths, verifies client-side enforcement through `client.complete()`, and demonstrates finish_reason/usage passthrough via an additive `meta` out-param.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test suite for the additive json_schema transport branch, verifying request-body nesting, live vLLM schema acceptance, preserved json_object path, client.complete enforcement, and finish_reason/usage meta passthrough.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a standalone, fail-loud acceptance harness that proves the json_schema transport branch is correctly shaped, accepted by the resident vLLM, backward-compatible, and observable without altering existing caller contracts.

symbols.does: Records a passing test and prints an ok message.
symbols.description: Simple helper that appends the test name to the global `_passed` list and prints a formatted success line.

symbols.does: Records a failing test with a reason and prints a failure message.
symbols.description: Helper that appends a `(name, why)` tuple to the global `_failed` list and prints a formatted failure line.

symbols.does: Builds a fake transport that records the request body for shape assertions.
symbols.description: Returns a callable transport that constructs the same body an OpenAI transport would send, applies `_apply_response_format`, and stores the resulting body in the supplied `captured` dict without making a network request.
Assembles a request body from model, messages, and options, applies response-format logic, and stores the body in the captured dict.

symbols.does: Unit-tests request-body response_format shapes for all transport option combinations.
symbols.description: Verifies json_schema nesting, json=True unchanged, schema= opt unchanged, free-text absence, json_schema precedence, and that both transports share `_apply_response_format`.

symbols.does: Probes the resident vLLM server at :8000 to decide whether live tests should run.
symbols.description: Attempts a short GET to `/models` and parses JSON; prints a skip message and returns False if the server is unreachable.

symbols.does: Live-tests that the resident vLLM accepts and returns schema-conformant JSON for a real FocusOut schema.
symbols.description: Calls the real transport with `json_schema`, validates the response as `FocusOut`, and runs a negative-control prompt to detect whether the backend is actually constraining decode.

symbols.does: Live-tests that the existing `json=True` path still works against the resident vLLM.
symbols.description: Sends a live request with `json=True` and asserts the response is still parseable JSON, proving the additive branch did not break the existing path.

symbols.does: Live-tests that `client.complete()` validates and retries over the json_schema branch.
symbols.description: Invokes `client.complete()` with a Pydantic schema and `json_schema`, confirming the enforcement layer returns a validated `FocusOut` instance even when json_schema is used.

symbols.does: Unit-tests that the `meta` out-param does not leak into the request body and is filled from the response envelope.
symbols.description: Uses a fake transport to assert `meta` is absent from the request body, that `_fill_meta` populates finish_reason and usage, and that omitting `meta` leaves the path unchanged.

symbols.does: Inner fake transport used by `test_meta_no_leak` to capture body and fill meta.
symbols.description: Builds a request body, applies response format and common options, captures the body, and calls `_fill_meta` with a synthetic response envelope.

symbols.does: Live-tests finish_reason and usage passthrough via the `meta` out-param.
symbols.description: Makes short and normal live calls to check `finish_reason='length'` and `'stop'`, verifies usage token counts, and confirms `complete()` still returns a validated model while filling `meta`.

symbols.does: Runs all unit and live tests, writes evidence, and exits non-zero on failure.
symbols.description: Orchestrates the acceptance suite, runs unit tests unconditionally, runs live tests only if the resident server is up, writes `/tmp/ltransport_evidence.json`, and reports pass/fail counts.
